mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in > 55% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or t-statistic on forward returns falls below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP growth and stable consumer price inflation indicates robust economic demand without overheating, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative returns in > 60% of cases or the interaction term loses statistical significance with p-value > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in the index of industrial production reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during recovery phases with negative 5d returns in > 50% of instances, or Rank IC drops below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 65% of high-inflation trigger months, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that supports corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns with a Rank IC below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or interaction effect is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in real personal spending reflect higher consumer demand and corporate revenue growth, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power with a t-statistic below 1.5 on out-of-sample forward returns, or Rank IC remains negative over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts exceeding 5.0% signals a sharp contraction in construction activity and broader economic weakness, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5d returns occur in > 55% of triggered instances despite the sharp housing decline, or the signal shows no statistical edge over random chance.
